summary: The reproduction built a pinned vulnerable Gotenberg image, exposed an internal-only secret service on the same Docker network, sent a crafted `/forms/pdfengines/embed` request with `downloadFrom`, and verified that the returned PDF contained the fetched internal response as an embedded attachment.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should cause Gotenberg to issue a backend GET request to an internal-only HTTP service chosen by the attacker and return that internal response to the attacker inside the output PDF as an embedded file.
actual_observed_result: Running `./reproduction/run.sh` cloned Gotenberg at commit `9bf12e7cb99f1ad70aee2e142fcba4750c3788fa` (`v8.30.1`), built the vulnerable image, started an internal-only secret server, posted a crafted request to `/forms/pdfengines/embed`, and extracted an embedded file named `internal-secret.txt` containing the expected proof string `EVOHUNT-SSRF-PROOF-c3d5b236` from the returned PDF.
verification_method: The verifier generates a seed PDF, sends it with a `downloadFrom` payload to `/forms/pdfengines/embed`, saves the returned PDF, extracts embedded attachments with `pypdf`, and checks that the extracted bytes contain the exact proof string. This distinguishes real backend fetch exfiltration from a generic PDF generation success, startup issue, or unrelated attachment.
evidence_produced_by_run: The returned PDF contained an embedded file named `internal-secret.txt`.
The extracted attachment bytes matched the exact proof string `EVOHUNT-SSRF-PROOF-c3d5b236`.
The verifier emitted a JSON result describing the target URL, secret URL, attachment name, and proof string for the successful run.
limitations_or_blockers: The `downloadFrom` implementation requires the fetched HTTP response to include a `Content-Disposition` header with a filename so the response can be materialized as a local file.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the public Gotenberg repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit from the `v8.30.1` line, builds the vulnerable image from that checkout, and mounts the checked-out repository into the helper container at `/target-repo`.
